title: Wireshark
description: The world's most popular network protocol analyzer — captures and inspects packets in real time.
fullExplanation: Wireshark captures raw packets from a network interface using libpcap (Linux/macOS) or WinPcap/Npcap (Windows). It decodes hundreds of protocols and displays them in a human-readable format. Wireshark is used for network troubleshooting, protocol learning, security analysis, and CTF challenges. It can read pcap files saved by tcpdump.
keyFeatures: Live packet capture from any network interface
Display filters to isolate specific traffic
Statistics: IO graphs, protocol hierarchy, conversations
Decrypt TLS if you have the session keys or RSA private key
Export objects (files transferred over HTTP, SMB, etc.)
keyFacts: Promiscuous mode: capture all packets on segment, not just your own
Monitor mode (WiFi): capture all 802.11 frames including management frames
TLS decryption: set SSLKEYLOGFILE env var in browser to log session keys
Coloring rules: TCP errors in red, HTTP in green by default
pcapng: next-gen capture format with metadata
